description_long: This is a standard urban traffic light mounted on a tall, black metal pole with a sturdy, square concrete base for stability. The signal head is painted yellow and features three circular lenses arranged vertically: red at the top, yellow in the middle, and green at the bottom. The lenses are housed in a robust, weather-resistant casing, with visors extending outward to shield the lights from sunlight and rain. The pole appears to be made of steel, and the base is likely reinforced concrete, providing significant weight and durability. The overall design is functional and utilitarian, suitable for placement at intersections or pedestrian crossings. No pedestrian signals or control boxes are visible, and the object is intended to be a fixed, immovable part of the urban environment.
description: A yellow, vertically-oriented traffic light with three colored lenses, mounted on a black metal pole with a concrete base. The design is robust and utilitarian, typical for urban intersections.
description_view_0: Side view showing the yellow signal head with three protruding visors and the black pole on a concrete base.
description_view_1: Front view displaying all three colored lenses (red, yellow, green) stacked vertically in the yellow housing.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the profile of the signal head and pole.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the yellow signal head and the full height of the black pole and concrete base.
